channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Telecoms boxes with open doors, all contents exposed and blinking, 2nd May
item.description:
The his cannot be safe, for information security or public safety. One of the doors actually states danger of death… please fix this as a matter of urgency.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Beechwood Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Takeaway litter and a suitcase, 2nd May
item.description:
Takeaway litter but now there's a large suitcase there in the garden area
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tottenham Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bollard uprooted, 2nd May
item.description:
Bollard uprooted and broken pavements

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tottenham Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Black bags flytipped, 2nd May
item.description:
Black bags have been flytipped on the pavement opposite-138 college road SE19 1XE

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): College Road, Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped items, 2nd May
item.description:
Outside Buckwheat House massive dumping of property items and cardboard boxes
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Maran Way, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Contents of bin on outside, 2nd May
item.description:
Lunchtime today. This regularly happens to this bin especially during warmer weather and people having picnics, etc. Animals are attracted overnightby food remains and pull litter out. The guy who empties the bin (does a very good job) must get fed up as bins are not fit for purpose nowadays. You can ask but some people will not take rubbish away if bin full. We need ones that shut and maybe operated by foot pedal. Please act before summer really is here and get appropriate bins. It’s a lovely well used space.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Queen's Mead Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Paint all over road, 2nd May
item.description:
Last week the parking bays white markings were painted. The contractors made a mess of rest of road with paint over top part of road in Stradella

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Stradella Road, Dulwich Village, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned Vehicle, 2nd May
item.description:
The Vehicle has been parked on double yellow lines on the corner of Lassa road with Everest road for over seven months without moving. It is displaying a Blue Disabled Badge, but the vehicle has not mover during that time, which is an illegal use of a Blue Disabled Badge. The vehicle is obstructing drivers view at the corner and is therefore causing a potential danger. The Vehicle also has more than one parking ticket on the windscreen.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Strongbow Crescent, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 2nd May
item.description:
Flytipping

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 159, Bestwood Street, New Cross Gate, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Furniture dumped in public alleyway in our street, 2nd May
item.description:
This furniture was dumped by a resident It is a whole sofa - lots of big cushions Stuff piled high The person who dumps it doesn’t often stay at the home apparently- just visits every now and again Since yesterday Friday 1 may

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Nursery Street, West Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sofa abandoned on pavement - blocking path, 2nd May
item.description:
A sofa has been flytipped on the pavement outside 33 Elliott Road. It is currently blocking the path.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pellis Yard, Widmore, Greater London

item.title: Restricted pavement, 2nd May
item.description:
Overhanging vegetation including sharp brambles next to a parking bay means the pavement is often restricted particularly for a wheelchair . 39 Sydney Road the house seems to have been unoccupied for several years.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sydney Road, Blendon, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
